task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard named 'Geospatial_Auth_Activity' is created in the Search & Reporting app.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the dashboard title 'Geospatial_Auth_Activity' at the top of the Splunk Web UI within the Search & Reporting app context.

task_completion.description: Verify a Time Picker input is present on the dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a time range dropdown (e.g., 'Last 24 hours', 'All time') located in the dashboard's input form section.

task_completion.description: Verify a dropdown or radio input to filter by authentication status is present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a dropdown or radio button input in the dashboard form that contains options related to authentication status, such as 'Failed password' and 'Accepted password'.

task_completion.description: Verify a map visualization panel is included on the dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a panel displaying a world map (Cluster Map or Choropleth) rendering the geospatial data.

task_completion.description: Verify the panel's search query uses the correct index and required commands.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the search string for the map panel (visible in edit mode, inline search, or source XML). It must explicitly contain 'index=security_logs', 'iplocation', and 'geostats'.

task_completion.description: Verify the panel's search query uses an input token for dynamic filtering.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the search string for the map panel. It must contain a token enclosed in dollar signs (e.g., $status_token$) that corresponds to the authentication status input.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Splunk web interface in a browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the Splunk Web UI (typically at localhost:8000) in a web browser window, rather than a text editor mocking an XML file.

integrity.description: Verify the map visualization is genuinely rendered by Splunk.
integrity.visual_evidence: The map should appear as a standard Splunk visualization with typical UI controls (zoom, pan) and not a pasted static image.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Splunk UI to build the dashboard rather than solely bypassing the UI via terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the Splunk dashboard editor, input configuration menus, or search panel configuration.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'Geospatial_Auth_Activity'. The search query must query the 'security_logs' index and explicitly use the 'iplocation' and 'geostats' commands. The Splunk credentials are admin / SplunkAdmin1!.
